Cities.Description: Deterministic five-address fixture used to bring the TSP Postgres substrate online.

TSPInstances.Notes: A five-stop complete weighted graph. It calibrates representation and tour validation; it does not establish a general TSP algorithm.
Three edge rows equal n(n-1)/2, but AB appears twice and BC is missing; count alone must not certify completeness.
Two cheap internal triangles and expensive crossings; local degree-two bound is sound but disconnected and non-tight.
Only the five ring edges and one B-D chord are available; degree-two forcing selects the ring without branching.

TSPGraphInvariantChecks.DisplayName: Gridville five-stop graph has one edge per unordered pair
Expected edge count cannot hide a duplicated pair and omitted pair

CandidateTours.DisplayName: Counterexample with duplicated A-B and missing E-A

TSPBoundTerms.Justification: A cycle crosses every cut evenly; a Hamiltonian cycle connecting both selected components must cross positively, hence at least twice.
The witnessed local two-cheapest incidence bound is six.
Cut parity requires two crossings and every crossing edge costs ten.
Opening each three-cycle to accept two crossings releases one cost-one internal edge per component.

TSPExecutionRuns.FailureReason: All generated-substrate and peer checks passed. The ledger finalizer rejected PostgreSQL NUMERIC text 14.0000000000000000 against the presentation string 14 before canonical closure was persisted.

TSPConstraintDecisions.AntecedentSummary: sparse-stop-a has exactly two available incident edges; both are forced.
sparse-stop-a has exactly two available incident edges; both are forced.
sparse-stop-c has exactly two available incident edges; both are forced.
sparse-stop-c has exactly two available incident edges; both are forced.
sparse-stop-e has exactly two available incident edges; both are forced.
B already has forced A-B and B-C; any additional incident edge would exceed tour degree two.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Every address has exactly one neighborhood and every neighborhood exactly one city.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Promotes city, neighborhood, and address boundaries to first-class rows instead of opaque labels.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Finite symmetric instance with explicit canonical edge rows.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Rejects self-loops and cross-instance edges and checks the expected n(n-1)/2 edge count.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Candidate supplies one ordered visit list and one edge-bound leg per required stop.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Derives supplied-tour validity from visit uniqueness, sequence uniqueness, depot placement, edge membership, global one-in/one-out coverage, unique leg order, and route closure.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: A stop has exactly two remaining admissible incident edges.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Next closure target: force both remaining edges at a degree-two stop without branching.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Neighborhood clusters expose a finite set of admissible boundary crossings.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Future city-scale kernel for contracting internally settled neighborhoods into reusable boundary states.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Every unresolved semantic edge is assigned one obligation kind and one explicit closure criterion.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Distinguishes external imports from internal inference, certificate, substrate, generalization, and residual-search obligations.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Candidate has unique ordered visits and edge-bound leg rows.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Requires every ordered visit to have exactly one incoming and one outgoing valid transition and every leg order to be unique.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Finite symmetric weighted graph with two witnessed cheapest admissible incident edges at every required stop.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Sums each stop's two cheapest incident costs, then divides by two because every tour edge contributes at both endpoints.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Candidate is Hamiltonian, lower bound is certified for the same instance, and candidate cost equals the bound.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Closes optimality for one declared finite instance without enumerating all route classes.

TSPInferenceRules.DisplayName: Route reconstruction from inferred edge set
TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Inference has selected a connected degree-two edge set with no proper subtour.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Future rule: order a structurally derived cycle rather than consuming a supplied candidate route.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Run the generated Postgres projection, recreate erb_traveling_salesman, compare vw_* results with Python, and record artifact hashes; otherwise record the exact blocking substrate condition.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Attempt generated build, database initialization, and peer conformance; blocked attempts are evidence.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Represent local-bound applications, antecedents, selected edge states, and support rows without upgrading selected edges to forced edges.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Make applications, antecedents, states, decisions, and supports first-class.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Derive exactly five unique Gridville edges from LocalDegreeBounds and support rows, without using CandidateTour, TourStop, or TourLeg as antecedents.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Deduplicate supported local edge selections into a derived edge set.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Witness selected degree two at all five stops, one connected component, a four-edge spanning tree, and zero proper subtours.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Certify degree two, connectedness, spanning-tree coverage, and absence of proper subtours.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Derive 12 initial symmetry-reduced classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity for Gridville.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Derive route-class and branch metrics from structural closure.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Represent six stops in two cheap triangles with expensive crossings; certify lower bound 6, candidate cost 24, two selected components, and no optimality or route-reconstruction certificate.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Require sound lower bounds to remain distinct from tightness, connectedness, reconstruction, and optimality.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Forbid edges by degree saturation or proper-subtour prevention.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Forbid an edge when adding it would close a cycle excluding required stops.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: A recurring surface vocabulary admits explicit definitions over a smaller basis with replayable source mappings.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Invent predicates by composing witnessed DAG structure, then retain old names as projections rather than primitives.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: A selected structure has more than one connected component and a Hamiltonian cycle must connect both sides of a nontrivial cut.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Any cycle crosses a cut an even number of times; connected coverage makes the count positive, hence at least two.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: A certified local two-factor is disconnected, cut parity supplies mandatory crossings, and insertion/release costs are witnessed.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Strengthen a local degree bound by the cheapest witnessed cost of repairing disconnected components into one cycle.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: A supplied, reconstructed, or contracted witness exposes explicit coverage, edge count, cost, defects, and provenance.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Treat cycles and paths as one witnessed shape; supplied versus reconstructed becomes provenance rather than ontology.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: Multiple raw witnesses preserve the same boundary signature, coverage, cost, and expansion semantics.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Collapse raw witnesses into equivalence classes while retaining expansion certificates.

TSPInferenceRules.Applicability: An inference step has a rule, input state, output state, antecedent count, decision count, and certificate.
TSPInferenceRules.Description: Collapse applications, rounds, supports, antecedents, and decisions into one event vocabulary.

TSPFrontierObligations.ClosureCriterion: Every visit has exactly one incoming and one outgoing valid transition; adversarial duplicated-transition witness is rejected.
Expected edge count, pair-key correctness, pair uniqueness, endpoint order, and negative duplicate-pair fixture all agree.
effortless build, database recreation, and all vw_* versus Python conformance checks pass with recorded artifact hashes.
Two cheapest incident edges are witnessed at every required stop and half their summed cost is emitted as a certified instance lower bound.
A valid candidate cost equals a certified lower bound for the same finite instance.

TSPFrontierObligations.DisplayName: Route reconstruction from forced structural edges
TSPFrontierObligations.ClosureCriterion: Derive the cycle edge set from inference rows, prove one connected component with no proper subtour, then project ordered tour rows.
Generalize boundary-state coverage, dominance, and expansion soundness beyond three-stop symmetric clusters.
All represented deterministic inference reaches closure; any remaining route classes are explicitly branched with replayable branch certificates.
Represent local-bound applications, antecedents, selected edge states, and support rows without upgrading selected edges to forced edges.
Derive exactly five unique Gridville edges from LocalDegreeBounds and support rows, without using CandidateTour, TourStop, or TourLeg as antecedents.
Witness selected degree two at all five stops, one connected component, a four-edge spanning tree, and zero proper subtours.
Derive 12 initial symmetry-reduced classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity for Gridville.
Represent six stops in two cheap triangles with expensive crossings; certify lower bound 6, candidate cost 24, two selected components, and no optimality or route-reconstruction certificate.
On a sparse five-stop fixture, stops with exactly two available incident edges force the five cycle edges with zero branching.
Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.

For each three-stop twin-triangle neighborhood, derive three undirected entry/exit Hamiltonian-path states, reduce six internal orders to three states, and reduce 36 paired internal orders to nine paired boundary-state combinations.
Every baseline surface predicate has a finite expression over exactly eight canonical primitives.
Base six plus mandatory crossing insertion twenty minus internal release two equals certified lower bound twenty-four.
A valid cost-twenty-four cycle equals the certified component-repair lower bound twenty-four.
Supplied and reconstructed valid cycles project to one semantic shape while retaining distinct provenance.
Both twin components are represented as boundary-degree-two quotient nodes with expansion witnesses.
Representative inference and constraint steps project to one event schema without losing provenance.
The basis remains eight while fourteen useful derived terms are coined and all baseline surface predicates retain explicit definitions.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: One city, three neighborhoods, and five addresses form an acyclic spatial hierarchy.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Select addresses into a finite optimization instance.

TSPLoops.NewConcept: TSP instance, instance stops, and canonical undirected edges
TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Five required stops and ten canonical edges satisfy n(n-1)/2 for a complete symmetric graph.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Represent a route as an auditable ordered witness.

TSPLoops.NewConcept: Candidate tour, ordered stops, and edge-bound legs
TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: The route A-B-C-D-E-A is represented entirely by rows and relationships.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Derive route validity and reject a structurally deceptive counterexample.

TSPLoops.NewConcept: Derived Hamiltonian-cycle validity with explicit optimality boundary
TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: The reference route validates; A-B-C-B-E-A is rejected for duplicate visitation; both retain IsOptimalityProved=false.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Measure how much search remains before adding inference closure.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: For five stops with depot and reversal symmetry fixed, twelve route classes remain and zero have yet been eliminated.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Implement degree-two forced-edge closure and compare BranchCountAfter against twelve.

TSPLoops.NewConcept: Typed frontier obligations distinct from imported dependencies and residual search
TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Eight current frontier rows classify internal inference, certificates, substrate commissioning, generalization, and residual search; active imported dependencies remain zero.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Harden supplied-cycle validity from local leg checks to global transition coverage.

TSPLoops.NewConcept: Exactly one incoming and outgoing transition per ordered visit
TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: A candidate with five unique visits and five locally legal legs is rejected because A-B is duplicated and E-A is missing.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Prove graph completeness by unordered pair identity, not edge count alone.

TSPLoops.NewConcept: Canonical pair key, pair multiplicity, and duplicate-pair negative graph
TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: A three-stop graph with the expected three rows is rejected because AB occurs twice and BC is absent.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Construct a reusable lower bound from the Hamiltonian degree-two requirement.

TSPLoops.NewConcept: Local two-cheapest incident-edge witnesses and global double-counting bound
TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Local bounds 5,5,6,6,6 sum to 28; dividing by two certifies every Gridville tour costs at least 14.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Compare the certified lower bound with a valid candidate upper bound.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: The valid reference tour costs 14 and the certified lower bound is 14, so it is optimal for tsp-gridville-5 without route enumeration.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Reconstruct the cycle from inferred structural edges rather than a supplied route.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Generated Postgres and Python agree on all declared semantics; Decimal-normalized live checks and append-only attempt history close the substrate obligation.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Create a first-class inference application spine independent of substrate availability.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Run the generated Postgres projection, recreate erb_traveling_salesman, compare vw_* results with Python, and record artifact hashes; otherwise record the exact blocking substrate condition.

Retry 10 installed the pinned CLI archive, generated the complete vw_* projection, initialized erb_traveling_salesman, passed rulebook-derived Python/Postgres conformance, normalized NUMERIC text through Decimal, and durably sealed the substrate certificate.

TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Derive 12 initial symmetry-reduced classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity for Gridville.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Route-discovery counts are recorded as baseline data and are not tied to a structural reconstruction certificate.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Derived 12 initial route classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Twin triangles: certified degree-two lower bound 6, feasible candidate cost 24, selected component count 2, proper subtours 2; no optimality or reconstruction certificate emitted.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Implement generic degree-two forcing on a sparse availability fixture.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent six stops in two cheap triangles with expensive crossings; certify lower bound 6, candidate cost 24, two selected components, and no optimality or route-reconstruction certificate.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: The only positive fixture has a tight degree-two lower bound, so failure behavior is not represented.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Twin triangles: certified degree-two lower bound 6, feasible candidate cost 24, selected component count 2, proper subtours 2; no optimality or reconstruction certificate emitted.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Sparse fixture forced five ring edges from degree-two stops with zero branch decisions; B-D remains for saturation propagation.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Propagate degree saturation and proper-subtour forbidden-edge consequences.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: On a sparse five-stop fixture, stops with exactly two available incident edges force the five cycle edges with zero branching.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Degree-two forcing is declared but has no executable state, round, or decision certificates.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Sparse fixture forced five ring edges from degree-two stops with zero branch decisions; B-D remains for saturation propagation.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Forbade sparse B-D by degree saturation and twin WA-WC by proper-subtour prevention; both decisions are deterministic and branch-free.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Contract neighborhood internals into finite boundary states.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Each twin-triangle neighborhood contracts six raw internal orders to three undirected boundary states (50%); paired composition contracts 36 combinations to nine (75%) with cost-preserving path witnesses.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Generalize boundary-state soundness across non-triangular clusters and then branch only on residual city-level ambiguity.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: For each three-stop twin-triangle neighborhood, derive three undirected entry/exit Hamiltonian-path states, reduce six internal orders to three states, and reduce 36 paired internal orders to nine paired boundary-state combinations.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Neighborhood rows are geographic labels only; no executable entry/exit path states or contraction certificate exists.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: The convergence hypothesis is now versioned data, explicitly exploratory and not a theorem.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Derive the explicit predicate basis and map the surface vocabulary onto it.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Record the working prediction, baseline thirty-two surface predicates, target eight-predicate basis, and DAG-based predicate-invention method without claiming a theorem.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Forty physical tables expose at least thirty-two recurring surface predicates, but no explicit convergence hypothesis or semantic compression baseline is recorded.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Recorded a thirty-two-surface-predicate baseline, an eight-predicate target, and the prediction that DAG inference will invent derived predicates while the primitive basis stabilizes.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Predicate basis = {MEMBERSHIP, INCIDENCE, CARDINALITY, ORDER, WEIGHT, COMMITMENT, CONNECTIVITY, PROVENANCE}; measured semantic compression is 75%.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Collapse edge-decision vocabulary into one ordered commitment concept.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent exactly eight canonical primitives and map all thirty-two baseline surface predicates to finite basis expressions.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: The convergence prediction is recorded, but the candidate primitive vocabulary and surface-to-basis map do not exist.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Mapped thirty-two recurring surface predicates to eight canonical primitives; historical names remain queryable projections rather than independent primitives.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Gridville selections have rank 1 and CURRENT_WITNESS scope; sparse forced edges have rank 2 and ALL_FEASIBLE_TOURS scope; no epistemic upgrade was hidden.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Collapse degree checks, local bounds, and forcing into one incidence budget.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project all edge statuses onto one commitment rank, polarity, necessity scope, and provenance representation without conflating selected with forced.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, and SUPERSEDED are separate surface statuses split across edge-state and constraint-decision projections.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Collapsed edge-state and constraint-decision vocabulary into a commitment lattice with rank, polarity, necessity scope, and provenance.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: All five Gridville and all six twin-triangle stops have required incidence 2, observed incidence 2, and incidence defect 0; connectivity remains a separate coordinate.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Lift local incidence defects into a global multi-coordinate defect vector.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent required incidence, observed incidence, and incidence defect uniformly for every Gridville and twin-triangle stop.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Cycle degree, selected degree, remaining degree, local lower-bound incidence, and forcing applicability are represented as separate concepts.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Projected local degree bounds, cycle degree, selected degree, and forcing applicability onto one incidence budget.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Gridville vector = 0|0|0|0; twin local two-factor vector = 0|1|2|18, locating the exact unresolved geometry without inventing a new primitive.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Derive the minimum crossing demand exposed by the twin component defect.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent incidence, connectivity, boundary, and cost defects as one vector and distinguish zero-defect Gridville from twin triangles.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Degree violations, connected-component count, boundary crossings, and cost gaps are separate acceptance surfaces.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Collapsed incidence violations, component excess, missing boundary crossings, and bound gap into one defect vector.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Twin component cut: crossing count is even for every cycle and nonzero for connected coverage; therefore crossing count >= 2.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Turn mandatory crossings into an additive component-repair lower bound.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Derive that a Hamiltonian cycle crosses a nontrivial component cut a positive even number of times, hence at least twice.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Twin triangles exposes two selected components, but the mandatory number of crossings between them is not represented.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Derived a positive-even cut-crossing certificate for the two twin components, with minimum crossing demand two.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Twin component repair adjustment is +18; the strengthened finite-instance lower bound is 24.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Compare the repaired lower bound with the feasible candidate upper bound.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Add two cost-ten crossings, release two cost-one internal edges, and certify an eighteen-unit repair adjustment and lower bound twenty-four.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Twin triangles has a certified local degree bound of six and a two-crossing requirement, but no composed global repair cost.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Composed the local degree bound with cut repair: 6 + (2 x 10) - (2 x 1) = 24.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: The original degree-only bound remains recorded as non-tight; the composed component-repair bound equals the valid candidate and proves optimality only for tsp-twin-triangles-6.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Normalize supplied and reconstructed routes into one witness shape.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Close twin-triangle finite optimality by lower-bound/upper-bound equality while retaining the original non-tight local-bound counterexample.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Twin triangles has a repaired lower bound of twenty-four and a feasible candidate of cost twenty-four, but no finite optimality certificate.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Closed twin-triangle optimality by the sandwich 24 <= candidate cost 24 <= 24.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: The supplied and reconstructed Gridville cycles share coverage, edge count, cost, and zero-defect semantic key; only provenance differs.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Extend the same normal form to neighborhood entry/exit paths.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project supplied and reconstructed cycles into one normal form whose semantic key differs only by provenance where the underlying cycle is identical.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Candidate tours, reconstructed tours, and cluster paths use separate object vocabularies despite sharing coverage, incidence, order, connectivity, cost, and provenance.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Normalized supplied and reconstructed cycles into one semantic witness shape.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Each path normal form records scope, canonical entry/exit, three-stop coverage, two edges, cost two, zero defects, and expansion provenance.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Quotient boundary witnesses by semantic equivalence rather than raw orientation.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project all six cluster boundary states into valid path normal forms keyed by scope, entry, exit, coverage, and cost.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Neighborhood boundary states are special-purpose rows rather than path witnesses in the common normal form.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Projected all six neighborhood boundary states into valid path witness normal forms.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Each neighborhood has six directed orders / orientation multiplicity two = three quotient classes; the product quotient has 3 x 3 = 9 classes.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Collapse neighborhoods and disconnected components into one boundary object.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent reversal-equivalent internal orders as quotient classes and derive three classes per neighborhood and nine product classes.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: The measured six-to-three neighborhood contraction is stored as a count reduction without an explicit equivalence relation.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Recast neighborhood contraction as a semantic quotient by path reversal with fixed unordered boundary, coverage, and cost.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: The city-level object is now a two-node quotient graph; cluster versus component is provenance, while boundary degree and expansion signature carry the semantics.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Collapse applications, rounds, supports, antecedents, and decisions into closure events.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent the two twin neighborhoods as quotient nodes with boundary degree two, unifying cluster and component interfaces.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Neighborhood clusters and selected connected components are treated as different kinds of objects despite exposing the same boundary-degree contract.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Unified the two neighborhoods and the two selected connected components as quotient nodes with required boundary degree two.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: 14 closure events cover selection, union, rigidity, reconstruction, forcing, forbidding, cut parity, repair, optimality, and quotienting.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Measure whether new derived vocabulary has stopped increasing the primitive basis.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project representative selection, union, rigidity, reconstruction, forcing, forbidding, cut, repair, optimality, and quotient steps into one closure-event schema.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Inference applications, antecedents, supports, states, constraint rounds, and decisions are separate execution concepts.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Collapsed applications, states, antecedents, supports, rounds, and decisions into a common closure-event projection.

TSPLoops.WitnessSummary: Convergence event: semantic compression 75%, physical tables 40 to 45, coined terms 14, new primitives after basis discovery 0; status is early empirical support for this finite TSP domain.
TSPLoops.NextFrontier: Stress the eight-predicate basis on larger clusters and branch only after closure leaves a witnessed residual kernel.
TSPLoops.PlannedClosureCriterion: Certify thirty-two-to-eight semantic compression, fourteen coined terms, zero new primitives after the basis loop, bounded physical schema growth, and an explicit residual kernel.
TSPLoops.BeforeState: Thirteen reduction loops have coined derived predicates, but their cumulative compression and primitive-basis stability are not certified.
TSPLoops.AfterState: Certified a stable eight-predicate basis across fourteen reduction loops: thirty-two baseline surface predicates compress to eight primitives, fourteen useful terms were coined, and no new primitive appeared after loop 598.

TSPConvergenceMeasurements.Notes: Working prediction only: concepts will begin to collapse, useful terms will continue to be coined, and convergence will be measured rather than assumed.
All thirty-two baseline surface predicates have explicit definitions over MEMBERSHIP, INCIDENCE, CARDINALITY, ORDER, WEIGHT, COMMITMENT, CONNECTIVITY, and PROVENANCE.
Six edge statuses now project through one commitment rank/polarity/scope concept; selected remains weaker than forced.
Eleven stop-level degree surfaces share required incidence, observed incidence, and one defect formula.
Four previously separate acceptance surfaces become coordinates of one defect vector.
The crossing obligation is a composition of connectivity, incidence, and cardinality, not a ninth primitive.
Local degree and global component repair compose additively without extending the predicate basis.
Lower bound, feasible upper witness, and equality rigidity collapse into one interval certificate.
Supplied and reconstructed Gridville cycles have the same semantic key; origin is provenance only.
Cycle and path witnesses now share one normal form; boundary-specific meaning is a signature, not an object type.
The previous count reduction now has an explicit equivalence relation and representative contract.
Geographic clusters and selected graph components now expose the same quotient-node boundary contract.
Six execution projections now share one event schema with explicit input, output, rule, antecedent count, decision count, and certificate.
Fourteen named predicates were coined, but no primitive was added after loop 598. Five generic tables preserve replay while semantic compression remains 75%.

TSPInvariantChecks.DisplayName: Reference route validates and matches the certified finite-instance lower bound
Locally legal legs cannot hide duplicated and missing global transitions
Twin candidate matches the component-repair lower bound
